effects.description: Flora summons a fast-moving vine against enemies, dealing Attack * 140% Area Damage and stuns the target for 2s.

effects.effect-name: Dealing Area Damage and Summon Adoria Rose
effects.description: Flora litters the target area with toxic Adoria bloom, dealing Attack * 40% Area Damage which sprouts into a flesh-eating Adoria Rose versus heroes. It inherits 30% of Flora’s attributes and attacks nearby enemies.
What does not kill the cultivator and the cultivated makes them stronger. Flora and summoned plants gain 10% Health and 20% Defense.

effects.effect-name: Granting All Troops a Chance to Increase Enemy Damage Taken
effects.description: Flora litters the ground with sharp vines to hobble enemy soldiers, granting all troops a 50% chance of increasing enemies’ Damage Taken by 50%.

effects.effect-name: Infantry Damage Reduction and Increase Joint Attack Damage
effects.description: Flora’s defensive foliage reduces Infantry Damage Taken by 25% while better Adoria Rose coordination with Lancers gives joint attacks dealing 25% extra damage.

3.trigger-point: Infantry Damage Taken and Marksman Damage Dealt

effects.effect-name: Infantry Damage Taken Increase and Marksman Damage Dealt Reduction
effects.description: Flora’s hallucinatory pollen increases enemy Infantry’s Damage Taken by 30%, and decreases enemy Marksmen’s Damage Dealt by 30% for 2 turns every 4 turns.

exploration.ability: Adoria Roses and vines can always be more toxic. Targets now suffer Attack * 25% damage every 0.5 seconds for 2 seconds.
exploration.name: Venom’s Heart

expedition.ability: The new fruit of Flora is highly rejuvenating, granting defending Troop 15% increased Health.
expedition.name: Fruit of Life
